text: him. Had he changed so much, already, that he was prepared to procreate and raise children? The thought of caring for human young was more unsettling than wandering the expansive wastes … Will and Ronin drifted between companionable silence and easy conversation as time passed. The automated machines took over after the internal repairs were completed, though Will remained at the controls to monitor their function. After his casing was sealed, Ronin entered the epidermal synthesizer — Will called it the reskinner , which seemed far more practical a name . As Ronin emerged from the reskinner, he glanced down at his torso. His skin was a single shade, now, result of his last visit to the Clinic. The realization came with a strange sense of loss . “Why did you perform the internal repairs by hand?” Ronin asked, pulling on his clothes. He’d been away from Lara for too long. The continued beeping of her heart monitor wasn’t enough to reassure him, but his curiosity was genuine . “Partly because it’s a skill that would be lost, otherwise. If we don’t know how to do it without machines to help, what’ll happen when these machines eventually break down? And for the same reason Doctor Cooper treats most of her patients by hand, I guess. It’s more…personal. If we don’t send our human patients to lie in a machine and receive care without ever seeing another person’s face, why should we do that to bots ?” While he walked back to Lara, Ronin analyzed his conversation with Will. Despite the mounting evidence, he had difficulty believing in the apparent truth of this place — here, bots and humans were considered equals. What-ifs would do him no good, but he couldn’t help wondering how things might have turned out if they’d known about this place sooner. How much trauma and pain could have been avoided ? He pulled open the curtain and stepped into the partition, raising his optics . A woman stood beside the bed, frowning down at Lara. She sighed . “There have been stories for a long time,” the woman said, turning to Ronin, “about this Warlord . Things he’s done. And, even though he was just a few miles away, it all seemed so unreal because we never saw it firsthand.” She ran a hand through her dark hair. The first signs of gray were visible at her temples. “This kind of brutality is unacceptable .” Ronin settled his gaze on Lara. “He did the same thing to her sister, but there was no one to help her in time .” The woman shook her head. “We’re not perfect here. Sometimes, fights break out. Every now and then a man hits his wife. Jack and his people get things back in order pretty quickly, but this …this is unlike anything I’ve ever seen .” She brushed her fingertips over the back of Lara’s hand, touch lingering for a second — enough for even Ronin to recognize the true compassion in the gesture. Drawing her hand away, she walked around the bed and held it out to Ronin . “I’m Nancy. Ronin, right ?” It took nearly a second to dredge up the data for an appropriate response. He took Nancy’s hand — as careful as ever to regulate the strength of his grip — and shook it. “Yes. You’re the Doctor Cooper everyone’s been talking about ?” “I am. The person with the drugs is always the most popular.” She smiled, but the expression wavered. “I’m sorry. I try to keep things light, but it’s inappropriate, given the situation .” “I understand. No apology necessary .” “We’re doing everything in our power to make her well again. Unfortunately, it’s just a waiting game , now .” “I know. Thank you, Nancy. Whatever happens.” Sorrow panged across his circuits at those last two words. Were they an admission of defeat? An acceptance of the possibility that she wouldn’t wake up, that their time together was done forever ? No. I won’t accept that . “She’s a fighter,” Nancy said. “I hear you are, too. That means a lot. Hell, if you hadn’t brought her here so fast, I don’t think she’d have survived the night .” They lapsed into silence, and Ronin watched Lara’s still form, a gentle ambience provided by the machinery . “I’ll give you some space,” Nancy said, after a minute. “You probably know already, but Jack’s not going to wait much longer to question you again. For now, I’m going to recommend that the best thing for Lara is to hear your voice and feel your touch. That’ll keep him at bay for a little while more .” She walked past him, pausing for a moment to place a hand on his shoulder and squeeze gently. Then she exited the partition, sliding the curtain closed behind her . It was jarring to face so much understanding, so much compassion, from so many people at once. He was a bot, but these people didn’t seem to make any distinction between metal and organic . He slid the chair over to the bed and sat, grasping Lara’s hand. None of that compassion would make a difference if she didn’t recover. Hers was the only touch he craved, the only understanding he needed . Chapter Twenty - Nine S he floated in darkness. It wracked her with agony, swallowed her, and she longed to cry out in pain. But she had no mouth, no lungs. There was nothing but her pain and the endless void . Until a voice penetrated the black . Don’t leave yet . Warmth bloomed through her, chasing away the icy cold for a fleeting moment. She knew that voice; it comforted her, shining like a beacon, giving her something to hold on to . When it spoke, shimmering blue flickered through the darkness, like the rolling waves of an ocean she’d never seen. But the light was gone so quickly it might
